title: Project closure-table relations
description: Generic closure-table substrate. One row per (parent, child, relation_type) edge. Multiple parallel hierarchies coexist as different relation_type values. Reassignment is a single field write. The schema is intentionally permissive on edge.parent's namespace — disambiguation between hierarchy edges (parent ∈ canonical ids) and lens edges (parent ∈ lens.bins) is cross-document and lives at SDK validation (validateRelations). Cycle detection lives at SDK traversal primitives (walkDescendants visited-set) and at validateRelations (edge_cycle_detected diagnostic per relation_type subgraph). Ships with the package as a framework-contract schema; user override at .project/schemas/relations.schema.json supersedes per the SDK's three-tier schema resolution.

parent.description: Either a canonical id (issue-NNN, FGAP-NNN) or a bin name from a lens definition. Mixing is intentional — a lens bin acts as a virtual parent node.

child.description: Canonical id of the member item.

relation_type.description: Lens id or hierarchy edge type. Allows multiple parallel hierarchies over the same items. Reserved relation_types for the roadmap/plan substrate per DEC-0012: 'phase_depends_on' (phase-A → phase-B ordering); 'plan_item_depends_on' (item-A → item-B ordering); 'phase_member' (phase-id → item-id membership); 'plan_member' (plan-id → item-id membership).

ordinal.description: Optional sort key for siblings under the same parent under the same relation_type. Ties broken by insertion order. Useful when a plan or roadmap wants explicit total ordering of independent items inside a phase or section.
